channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources worldwide

item.title: 🇯🇵🏦 Bank of Japan Governor Hospitalized, Will Miss Key Policy Meeting
item.description: BOJ Governor Kazuo Ueda has been hospitalized for medical treatment and will miss the June 15-16 policy meeting, where the central bank was expected to raise interest rates to 1.0% amid inflation concerns.

item.title: 🚢💰 Iran War Fears Double Container Shipping Rates from Asia to US
item.description: The cost of shipping a container from Asia to the United States has doubled since the start of the Iran war, driven by spiking fuel prices and disruptions to global trade routes through the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: ⚔️🇮🇷 Trump Vows More Strikes on Iran After Exchange of Attacks
item.description: The US launched airstrikes on Iranian military sites after Tehran shot down an American Apache helicopter near the Strait of Hormuz. Trump threatened to hit them hard again if no peace deal is reached, as back-and-forth attacks threaten an April ceasefire.

item.title: 🚢🇺🇦 Two Merchant Vessels Damaged in Ukrainian Black Sea Corridor Attack
item.description: A Panamanian-flagged and a Barbadian-flagged vessel were damaged while transiting Ukraine's Black Sea navigation corridor, highlighting ongoing risks to commercial shipping despite the corridor's establishment.

item.title: 📈🛒 US Inflation Surges to 4.2% as Iran War Drives Energy Price Shock
item.description: Consumer prices rose at 4.2% annually in May, the fastest pace in three years, as the Iran conflict drove energy costs sharply higher and rippled through the broader economy.

item.title: 🇮🇳⚔️ India Accelerates Military Revamp to Counter China and Pakistan
item.description: India is moving forward with Integrated Theatre Commands that would unite the army, navy and air force in its biggest military shake-up in decades, reflecting growing concerns about simultaneous two-front threats.

item.title: ☢️🇮🇷 IAEA Board Passes Resolution Demanding Iran Report Uranium Stocks
item.description: The UN nuclear watchdog's 35-nation Board of Governors passed a US-backed resolution telling Iran to declare its remaining uranium stocks, as concerns grow about Iran's nuclear program amid the ongoing conflict.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇯🇵 US Tungsten Scrap Exports to Japan Soar on Chinese Curbs
item.description: Japan-bound US shipments of recycled tungsten have surged dramatically as China restricts exports of the critical industrial metal, highlighting supply chain vulnerabilities in strategic materials.

item.title: 🇮🇱🏛️ Israeli Lawmakers Advance Bill to Exempt Ultra-Orthodox from Military Service
item.description: Israeli MPs voted to advance legislation that would allow most ultra-Orthodox Jewish men to avoid conscription, a controversial move amid ongoing war and domestic political tensions.

item.title: 🛢️🚢 US Forces Disable Oil Tanker in Gulf of Oman for Blockade Violation
item.description: US Central Command announced that its forces disabled a Palau-flagged oil tanker transiting toward Iran for alleged violation of the blockade, marking the latest enforcement action in the Gulf.

item.title: 🌍⚠️ UN Chief Warns of Risk of 'Full War' Return in Middle East
item.description: UN Secretary-General Antonio Guterres warned that the Middle East risks returning to full-scale war as US-Iran tensions escalate, urging all parties to exercise maximum restraint.

item.title: 📈💵 US Consumer Prices Increase Expected for May as Inflation Pressures Build
item.description: US consumer prices were expected to show another increase for May as energy costs continue to rise amid the Iran conflict, with economists watching for signs of entrenched inflation.

item.title: 🌏💸 Foreign Outflows from Asian Equities Surge on Middle East Risks
item.description: Foreign investors have pulled billions from Asian equity markets as Middle East conflict risks and a global tech rout drive risk-off sentiment across emerging market assets.

item.title: 🇯🇵💹 Bank of Japan Set to Raise Key Rate to 1.0% in June, Poll Shows
item.description: The Bank of Japan will raise its key interest rate this month and again in the fourth quarter, taking borrowing costs to 1.25% by year-end, as it grows more wary of inflation risks, a Reuters poll of economists showed.

item.title: 🇱🇧💥 Israeli Airstrikes Kill 13 in South Lebanon
item.description: Israeli airstrikes in southern Lebanon killed at least 13 people on Wednesday, Lebanese security sources said, as Israel pressed its campaign against Hezbollah and the Iran-backed group claimed fresh attacks against Israeli forces.

item.title: 🇲🇲🔍 Thai Woman in Custody After US Diplomat Found Dead in Myanmar
item.description: A Thai woman has been taken into custody after an American diplomat was found dead in Myanmar, with authorities investigating the circumstances of the death.

item.title: 🇵🇰🚁 Pakistan Military Helicopter Crash Kills All Aboard in Kashmir
item.description: All personnel on board a military helicopter have been killed in a crash near Muzaffarabad in Pakistani Kashmir, Pakistan's military said in a statement, without specifying the number of deaths.

item.title: 🇺🇦🇷🇺 Russia Targets NATO Countries in Drone Campaign, Reports Show
item.description: Russia has been targeting NATO countries including Lithuania and Romania with drones in an expanded campaign that risks drawing the alliance deeper into the Ukraine conflict.

item.title: 🛢️📉 OPEC Oil Output Hits 25-Year Low as US Blockade Squeezes Iran
item.description: OPEC oil production has fallen to its lowest level since at least 2000, a Reuters survey showed, as the US naval blockade sharply reduces Iranian exports and tightens global supply.

item.title: 🇹🇼🇨🇳 Taiwan Fires Rockets from US-Supplied HIMARS in China-Direction Drill
item.description: Taiwan's military has sent rockets in China's direction from US-supplied HIMARS launchers in a demonstration of how it might defend against a potential invasion from Beijing.

item.title: 🇬🇧🗳️ Graham Platner Wins Maine Primary, Setting Up Face-Off with Collins
item.description: Voters headed to the polls in primary elections in Maine, Nevada, North Dakota, and South Carolina ahead of the midterms, with Graham Platner winning the Democratic Senate primary in Maine.

item.title: ⚔️🇮🇷 CBS News: Trump Orders Strikes on Iran After Apache Helicopter Downed
item.description: President Trump ordered strikes on Iranian targets after Tehran shot down a US Apache helicopter near the Strait of Hormuz, with CBS News providing live updates on the escalating conflict.

item.title: 🌍🍚 Iran War Could Spark Global Hunger Crisis, World Food Program Warns
item.description: The World Food Program has warned that the Iran conflict could trigger a global hunger crisis as food prices rise and supply chains are disrupted by the ongoing hostilities.

item.title: 🇶🇦💬 Qatari Negotiators Travel to Tehran to Bridge Gaps in US-Iran Talks
item.description: Qatari mediators have traveled to Tehran in an effort to narrow differences between Washington and Tehran as negotiations stall and military tensions escalate.

item.title: 🌐💾 EU and South Korea Deepen Ties with Digital Trade Pact
item.description: The European Union and South Korea signed a digital trade agreement designed to make digital transactions easier and further cement economic ties between the partners at their first summit in three years.

item.title: 🇺🇸📈 US Consumer Prices Rise 4.2% in May as Iran War Drives Inflation Higher
item.description: US consumer prices rose 4.2% annually in May, exceeding expectations and marking the fastest pace in three years as the Iran conflict drove energy costs sharply higher.
